testcase.name: test_14_10_memory_controller_parameters_receive_gradients

failure.message: AssertionError: eviction_controller received no finite gradient. assert False + where False = any(<generator object test_14_10_memory_controller_parameters_receive_gradients.<locals>.<genexpr> at 0x7f1bffda0ee0>)

testcase.failure:
def test_14_10_memory_controller_parameters_receive_gradients() -> None: model = build_model(seed=2026, budget=4, force_detach=False) model.train() encourage_writes(model) x = compatible_input(model, seq_len=16) model.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
output = invoke(model, x, reset=True)
controller_terms = [] for names in ( ("write_probabilities",), ("auxiliary_mean",), ("auxiliary_log_variance",), ("retrieval_weights",), ): tensor = extract_named_tensor(output, names) if ( tensor is not None and tensor.dtype.is_floating_point and tensor.requires_grad ): controller_terms.append(tensor.float().mean())
primary = extract_tensor(output) if ( primary is not None and primary.dtype.is_floating_point and primary.requires_grad ): controller_terms.append(primary.float().pow(2).mean())
assert controller_terms, ( "No differentiable controller diagnostic or model output was exposed." )
loss = torch.stack(controller_terms).sum() loss.backward()
parameters = controller_parameters(model) assert parameters, "No controller parameters were discovered."
finite_gradient_names = [ name for name, parameter in parameters if parameter.grad is not None and torch.isfinite(parameter.grad).all() ] nonzero_gradient_names = [ name for name, parameter in parameters if parameter.grad is not None and torch.count_nonzero(parameter.grad).item() > 0 ]
assert finite_gradient_names, ( "No memory-controller parameter received a finite gradient." ) assert nonzero_gradient_names, ( "All observed memory-controller gradients were zero." )
expected_families = ( "write_controller", "eviction_controller", "initial_utility_head", ) for family in expected_families: family_parameters = [ name for name, _ in parameters if family in name ] if not family_parameters: continue > assert any( name in finite_gradient_names for name in family_parameters ), f"{family} received no finite gradient." E AssertionError: eviction_controller received no finite gradient. E assert False E + where False = any(<generator object test_14_10_memory_controller_parameters_receive_gradients.<locals>.<genexpr> at 0x7f1bffda0ee0>)
tests/test_section14_required.py:345: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_strict_budget_is_never_violated

failure.message: ValueError: only one element tensors can be converted to Python scalars

testcase.name: test_eval_is_deterministic
testcase.failure:
self = {'logits': tensor([[ 0.1704, -0.0095, 0.0932], [ 0.2229, 0.0051, 0.1016], [ 0.2046, -0.0101, 0.069...ensor([6, 6, 6])), 'memory_mask': tensor([[True, True, True], [True, True, True], [True, True, True]])} name = 'write_slots'

src/budgetmem/models/budgetmem_r.py:29: KeyError
The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
def test_eval_is_deterministic() -> None: torch.manual_seed(2026) model = _model(write_threshold=0.5).eval() inputs = torch.randn(3, 7, 6)
first = model(inputs, budget=3) second = model(inputs, budget=3)
assert torch.equal(first.hard_writes, second.hard_writes) > assert torch.equal(first.write_slots, second.write_slots) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

failure.message: ValueError: only one element tensors can be converted to Python scalars

testcase.name: test_budget_sampler_uses_only_allowed_values
test_invalid_budget_is_rejected

failure.message: AssertionError: Regex pattern did not match. Expected regex: 'requested budget' Actual message: 'requested_budget=6 is not in allowed_budgets=(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)'

testcase.failure:
def test_invalid_budget_is_rejected() -> None: model = _model().eval() inputs = torch.randn(2, 4, 6) > with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="requested budget"): E AssertionError: Regex pattern did not match. E Expected regex: 'requested budget' E Actual message: 'requested_budget=6 is not in allowed_budgets=(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)'
tests/models/test_budgetmem_r.py:116: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_memory_never_exceeds_configured_budget_at_any_forward_step
testcase.failure:
self = {'logits': tensor([[ 0.0233, 0.2921, 0.2467], [ 0.0085, 0.1934, 0.2687], [-0.0143, 0.2097, 0.311...sor([1, 1, 1]), last_write_step=tensor([11, 11, 11])), 'memory_mask': tensor([[True], [True], [True]])} name = 'memory_masks'
def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> object: try: > return self[name] ^^^^^^^^^^ E KeyError: 'memory_masks'
src/budgetmem/models/budgetmem_r.py:29: KeyError
The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
def test_memory_never_exceeds_configured_budget_at_any_forward_step() -> None: model = build_budgetmem_model().eval() inputs = make_inputs(model, batch_size=3, sequence_length=12)
with torch.no_grad(): for budget in allowed_budgets(model): output = model(inputs, budget=budget) expected_budgets = output.budgets.unsqueeze(1).expand_as( output.memory_sizes )
assert torch.all(output.memory_sizes <= expected_budgets) assert torch.equal( > output.memory_masks.sum(dim=-1), ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ output.memory_sizes, )
tests/pretraining/test_budget_and_causality.py:27: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _

testcase.name: test_future_tokens_do_not_change_earlier_memory_decisions
testcase.failure:
self = {'logits': tensor([[ 0.2492, -0.2311, 0.0992], [ 0.2719, -0.2766, 0.0904]]), 'sequence_logits': tensor([[[ 0... [True]]), budgets=tensor([1, 1]), last_write_step=tensor([10, 10])), 'memory_mask': tensor([[True], [True]])} name = 'hidden_states'
def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> object: try: > return self[name] ^^^^^^^^^^ E KeyError: 'hidden_states'
src/budgetmem/models/budgetmem_r.py:29: KeyError
The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
def test_future_tokens_do_not_change_earlier_memory_decisions() -> None: model = build_budgetmem_model().eval() inputs = make_inputs(model, batch_size=2, sequence_length=11, seed=7001) changed = inputs.clone() cutoff = 6
generator = torch.Generator(device="cpu") generator.manual_seed(7002) changed[:, cutoff:, :] = torch.randn( changed[:, cutoff:, :].shape, generator=generator, )
budget = allowed_budgets(model)[0] with torch.no_grad(): original = model(inputs, budget=budget) perturbed = model(changed, budget=budget)
floating_prefixes = ( (original.sequence_logits, perturbed.sequence_logits), > (original.hidden_states, perturbed.hidden_states), ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ (original.write_probabilities, perturbed.write_probabilities), (original.retrieval_weights, perturbed.retrieval_weights), )

testcase.name: test_same_seed_produces_identical_model_initialization_and_evaluation
testcase.failure:
self = {'logits': tensor([[ 0.1366, -0.0607, 0.1002], [ 0.2422, 0.0922, 0.1546]]), 'sequence_logits': tensor([[[ 0... [True]]), budgets=tensor([1, 1]), last_write_step=tensor([9, 9])), 'memory_mask': tensor([[True], [True]])} name = 'hidden_states'
def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> object: try: > return self[name] ^^^^^^^^^^ E KeyError: 'hidden_states'
src/budgetmem/models/budgetmem_r.py:29: KeyError
The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
def test_same_seed_produces_identical_model_initialization_and_evaluation() -> None: seed_everything(2026) first_model = build_budgetmem_model().eval() first_state = { name: tensor.detach().clone() for name, tensor in first_model.state_dict().items() }
seed_everything(2026) second_model = build_budgetmem_model().eval() second_state = second_model.state_dict()
assert first_state.keys() == second_state.keys() for name in first_state: assert torch.equal(first_state[name], second_state[name])
inputs = make_inputs(first_model, batch_size=2, sequence_length=10, seed=8080) budget = allowed_budgets(first_model)[0] with torch.no_grad(): first_output = first_model(inputs, budget=budget) second_output = second_model(inputs, budget=budget)
for name in ( "logits", "sequence_logits", "hidden_states", "write_probabilities", "hard_writes", "write_slots", "eviction_flags", "retrieval_weights", "memory_masks", "memory_sizes", "budgets", ): assert torch.equal( > getattr(first_output, name), ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ getattr(second_output, name), )
tests/pretraining/test_determinism.py:100: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _

testcase.name: test_memory_controllers_receive_gradients_and_graph_policy_is_explicit
testcase.failure:
self = {'logits': tensor([[-0.2411, -0.0705, 0.2880], [-0.2574, -0.0537, 0.2297]], grad_fn=<SelectBackward0>), 'seq... [True]]), budgets=tensor([1, 1]), last_write_step=tensor([9, 9])), 'memory_mask': tensor([[True], [True]])} name = 'eviction_flags'
def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> object: try: > return self[name] ^^^^^^^^^^ E KeyError: 'eviction_flags'
src/budgetmem/models/budgetmem_r.py:29: KeyError
The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
def test_memory_controllers_receive_gradients_and_graph_policy_is_explicit() -> None: # Test the write controller under its normal, nonsaturated initialization. torch.manual_seed(12000) write_model = build_budgetmem_model().train()
inputs = make_inputs( write_model, batch_size=2, sequence_length=10, seed=12001, ) budget = allowed_budgets(write_model)[0] write_output = write_model(inputs, budget=budget)
write_loss = write_output.write_probabilities.mean() write_loss.backward()
assert _has_nonzero_gradient(write_model.write_controller)
# Use a separate model to force writes and evictions. The forced controller # is intentionally saturated, so its gradient is not tested in this pass. torch.manual_seed(12000) memory_model = build_budgetmem_model().train() _force_controller_to_write(memory_model)
memory_output = memory_model(inputs, budget=budget)
assert bool(torch.all(memory_output.hard_writes)) > assert bool(torch.any(memory_output.eviction_flags)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/pretraining/test_gradient_and_reset.py:72: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = {'logits': tensor([[-0.2411, -0.0705, 0.2880], [-0.2574, -0.0537, 0.2297]], grad_fn=<SelectBackward0>), 'seq... [True]]), budgets=tensor([1, 1]), last_write_step=tensor([9, 9])), 'memory_mask': tensor([[True], [True]])} name = 'eviction_flags'
def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> object: try: return self[name] except KeyError as exc: > raise AttributeError(name) from exc E AttributeError: eviction_flags
src/budgetmem/models/budgetmem_r.py:31: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_memory_is_reset_between_unrelated_forward_calls
testcase.failure:
self = {'logits': tensor([[-0.3065, -0.1060, -0.2027], [-0.2677, -0.2257, -0.2088]]), 'sequence_logits': tensor([[[-0... [True]]), budgets=tensor([1, 1]), last_write_step=tensor([8, 8])), 'memory_mask': tensor([[True], [True]])} name = 'hidden_states'
def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> object: try: > return self[name] ^^^^^^^^^^ E KeyError: 'hidden_states'
src/budgetmem/models/budgetmem_r.py:29: KeyError
The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
def test_memory_is_reset_between_unrelated_forward_calls() -> None: model = build_budgetmem_model().eval() fresh_model = copy.deepcopy(model).eval() budget = allowed_budgets(model)[0]
unrelated = make_inputs( model, batch_size=2, sequence_length=7, seed=13001, ) target = make_inputs( model, batch_size=2, sequence_length=9, seed=13002, )
with torch.no_grad(): model(unrelated, budget=budget) after_unrelated = model(target, budget=budget) fresh = fresh_model(target, budget=budget)
for name in ( "logits", "sequence_logits", "hidden_states", "write_probabilities", "hard_writes", "write_slots", "eviction_flags", "retrieval_weights", "memory_masks", "memory_sizes", "budgets", ): assert torch.equal( > getattr(after_unrelated, name), ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ getattr(fresh, name), )
tests/pretraining/test_gradient_and_reset.py:129: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = {'logits': tensor([[-0.3065, -0.1060, -0.2027], [-0.2677, -0.2257, -0.2088]]), 'sequence_logits': tensor([[[-0... [True]]), budgets=tensor([1, 1]), last_write_step=tensor([8, 8])), 'memory_mask': tensor([[True], [True]])} name = 'hidden_states'
def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> object: try: return self[name] except KeyError as exc: > raise AttributeError(name) from exc E AttributeError: hidden_states
src/budgetmem/models/budgetmem_r.py:31: AttributeError

testcase.name: test_imdb_official_test_examples_are_not_in_train_or_validation

failure.message: pyarrow.lib.ArrowInvalid: No match for FieldRef.Name(source_index) in __fragment_index: int32 __batch_index: int32 __last_in_fragment: bool __filename: string

testcase.failure:
def test_imdb_official_test_examples_are_not_in_train_or_validation() -> None: root = REPO_ROOT / "data" / "processed" / "imdb" paths = { "train": root / "train" / "data.parquet", "validation": root / "validation" / "data.parquet", "test": root / "test_locked" / "data.parquet", } manifest_path = REPO_ROOT / "data" / "manifests" / "imdb_manifest.json" indices_path = REPO_ROOT / "data" / "manifests" / "imdb_split_indices.json"
required = [*paths.values(), manifest_path, indices_path] if not all(item.exists() for item in required): pytest.skip("IMDb data or provenance manifests are not all present")
> train = pd.read_parquet(paths["train"], columns=["source_index"]) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/pretraining/test_split_leakage.py:119: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ .venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/pandas/io/parquet.py:671: in read_parquet return impl.read( .venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/pandas/io/parquet.py:260: in read pa_table = self.api.parquet.read_table( .venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/pyarrow/parquet/core.py:1964: in read_table return dataset.read(columns=columns, use_threads=use_threads, .venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/pyarrow/parquet/core.py:1590: in read table = self._dataset.to_table( pyarrow/_dataset.pyx:579: in pyarrow._dataset.Dataset.to_table ??? pyarrow/_dataset.pyx:415: in pyarrow._dataset.Dataset.scanner ??? pyarrow/_dataset.pyx:3706: in pyarrow._dataset.Scanner.from_dataset ??? pyarrow/_dataset.pyx:3619: in pyarrow._dataset.Scanner._make_scan_options ??? pyarrow/_dataset.pyx:3569: in pyarrow._dataset._populate_builder ??? _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
> ??? E pyarrow.lib.ArrowInvalid: No match for FieldRef.Name(source_index) in __fragment_index: int32 E __batch_index: int32 E __last_in_fragment: bool E __filename: string
pyarrow/error.pxi:92: ArrowInvalid
